question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/contractController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getDetailedContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a valid contractId and consensusTimestamp that resolves to a real contract, triggering Promise.all() to fire five simultaneous expensive DB queries (getContractResultsByTimestamps, getEthTransactionByTimestampAndPayerId, getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestamp, getContractLogsByTimestamps, getContractStateChangesByTimestamps) at high request rate, exhausting the database connection pool and causing all new transaction confirmations to stall?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/contractController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: optimizeTimestampFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp range filter (e.g., timestamp=gte:0&timestamp=lte:9999999999.999999999) that causes bindTimestampRange() to perform a full-table scan on the record_file table, and by flooding this endpoint concurrently, exhaust DB CPU and I/O such that the mirror node cannot process incoming consensus transactions?
